title: [CI:DOCS] upload a translation file
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

it is a simplified chinese translation editon for the podman_tutorial.md
<!---
Please put your overall PR description here
-->

#### How to verify it

<!---
Please specify the precise conditions and/or the specific test(s) which must pass.
-->
None
#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:
None
<!--
Please uncomment this block and include only one of the following on a
line by itself:

None

-OR-

Fixes #<issue number>

*** Please also put 'Fixes #' in the commit and PR description***

-->

#### Special notes for your reviewer:
None

comments.body: Thanks @of2x Are their any Chinese readers out their to proof read this?  If not we can just merge once it is signed.
@chuanchang @ypu if either of you could take a quick look at this, it would be appreciated.
@of2x 非常感谢！  In general, looks good to me, but have asked a couple of our Chinese team members to take a look at it.
@of2x Thanks a lot for the translation. Overall it looks very good. Just left some small comments in the file for some words to make it more suitable for the habit of programmer.

Hi @of2x left some comments in the file. And have a small advise about the pullreq. As the three commits are fix one problem, it may be better to combine them to one commit, and use git push --force to update it to your branch. 

And as @vrothberg 's comments in the pullreq, you also need setup your git config following:
https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/setting-up-and-managing-your-github-user-account/managing-email-preferences/setting-your-commit-email-address#setting-your-commit-email-address-in-git
https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/getting-started-with-git/setting-your-username-in-git

And generate the commit with:
git commit -s ...

So we can see your email and name in the commit message. And your work is recorded more clearly in the project.

> @ypu TYVM for the review!

My pleasure. :)
@of2x looks like you have some trailing whitespace causing the validate test to fail. Please fix that and we can get this merged

Probably a little late in life to learn Chinese.
/lgtm
/approve
Thanks @of2x

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *of2x*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@of2x Interested in translating Man pages, or do they move too quickly?
